meta.label: Levantine Lentil Soup (Shorbat Adas)
meta.description: Classic Levantine lentil soup with red lentils, aromatics, cumin, and lemon. Smooth or lightly chunky, naturally vegan, pantry-friendly, and ideal for meal prep.

summary: Traditional Levantine red lentil soup (Shorbat Adas) built on onion-carrot aromatics, cumin, and a bright lemon finish. Red lentils cook quickly and naturally thicken the soup; blend fully for a silky texture or blend partially for a rustic bowl. Lemon at the end is essential for the classic flavor.

timing.notes: Blending is optional and adds a few minutes.

diet.notes: Gluten-free as written. Serve with GF bread if desired.

compat.notes: If you don't have a blender, mash with a potato masher for a thicker texture.

foodSafetyNotes: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat until steaming hot throughout; thin with water/stock as needed.

ingredients.label: Ground turmeric (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 tsp for color and warmth.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1 Tbsp. Adds depth and color; omit for a more classic plain version.

ingredients.label: Red lentils
ingredients.notes: Rinse well until water runs mostly clear; pick out debris.

ingredients.label: Water or vegetable stock
ingredients.notes: Start here; add more to thin as needed.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start here; adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: About 3 Tbsp; add to taste at the end.

steps.title: Sauté the aromatics
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and carrots with a pinch of salt. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant (do not brown).

steps.title: Bloom spices (and tomato paste if using)
instructions: Add cumin (and turmeric/paprika if using). Stir 20–30 seconds to bloom.
If using tomato paste, add it now and cook 1 minute, stirring, to deepen flavor.

steps.title: Add lentils and simmer
instructions: Add rinsed red lentils, water/stock, salt, black pepper, and bay leaf (optional).
Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Simmer 18–22 minutes until lentils are fully soft and the soup is naturally thickened.

steps.title: Blend (optional) for texture
instructions: For silky soup: blend until smooth using an immersion blender (in the pot) or a countertop blender (work in batches).
For rustic soup: blend briefly or mash with a potato masher.
If too thick, add a little water/stock and stir until desired consistency.

steps.title: Finish with lemon
instructions: Stir in lemon juice (and zest if using). Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon.
Optional: drizzle with olive oil and garnish with parsley/cilantro.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with pita or croutons (optional).
Store leftovers refrigerated 3–4 days; the soup thickens as it sits—thin with water when reheating.
